calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For Relief Under Probate Code 4541, Including Revocation Of Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Off Calendar

PETITION Petition For Relief Under Probate Code 4541, Including Revocation Of Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Continued To Feb-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue To Alameda County
Off Calendar Jan-21-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Dimitriou, Esq. appearing for Vincent Crisostomo, moving party.
J. Allison Tussey, Esq. appearing for

Matter called. Argument on Respondent's Motion to Transfer Venue To Alameda County is held.
Mr. Dimitriou's objection to argument by Ms. Tussey is overruled by the Court. Argument resumes.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative as follows: Two issues are before the Court, Respondent's motion to transfer venue to Alameda County, and his request for sanctions. This tentative ruling will address each in turn.The motion to transfer venue appears to have been rendered moot by Petitioner's request to dismiss the pending petition in San Francisco filed January 19, 2021. The Court is in receipt of the request to dismiss, as well as the January 19, 2021 letter from Respondent's counsel to the Director of Probate asking that the motion not be removed from the calendar as moot, and specifically seeking a ruling on the merits of his sanctions request. Unless the Probate Code provides otherwise, the rules of practice applicable to civil actions apply to probate proceedings. (Prob. C. section 1000.) Generally, a petitioner has the right to enter a voluntary dismissal under Code of Civil Procedure section 581. (See Estate of Cook (1928) 205 Cal. 581, 587.) The Court intends to dismiss the petition as requested, but only after adjudicating the sanctions request on the merits. Respondent's sanctions request is DENIED. Under CCP section 396b(b), the Court may order payment of reasonable expenses and attorney's fees to the party making or opposing a motion to transfer and from the opposing attorney personally, depending upon: "(1) whether an offer to stipulate to change of venue was reasonably made and rejected, and (2) whether the motion or selection of venue was made in good faith given the facts and law the party making the motion or selecting the venue knew or should have known. As between the party and his or her attorney, those expenses and fees shall be the personal liability of the attorney not chargeable to the party." (CCP section 396b(b).) Here, it was not clearly erroneous to bring a petition in San Francisco regarding the powers of attorney. As Respondent acknowledges, the action was filed in the county where he as the named agent resides, in keeping with Probate Code sections 4523 and 4763. Even in the letter from Respondent's counsel to Petitioner's counsel, there is an acknowledgment that the petitions filed in this county were not strictly improper: "From what we understand, your client's petitions in San Francisco seek relief that is more properly sought by [sic] an Alameda County Court." (November 5, 2020 letter, second paragraph, first sentence.) In conclusion, the Court finds that the offer to stipulate to change in venue was not unreasonably rejected, and that the selection of San Francisco for the Petition on matters relating to acts and transactions of the agent or attorney-in-fact was not made in bad faith based on the face of the relevant statutes (Probate sections 4523, 4763). Because the offer to stipulate to the change of venue was not unreasonably rejected, and there is no showing that the petition was not brought in good faith based on the facts and the law, the Court declines to order the requested sanctions. (CCP section 396b(b)).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Posting Of The Tentative Ruling
Off Calendar Jan-21-2021 Text Ruling

PETITION Petition For Relief Under Probate Code 4541, Including Revocation Of Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Allison Tussey appeared by zoom for and with Francine Crisostomo (Petitioner) by zoom.
Andrew Dimitriou appeared by zoom for Vincent Crisostomo (Objector).
Clodagh Byrne appeared by zoom.

The Court noted that Mr. Dimitriou tried to file something, but was unsuccessful. Mr. Dimitriou stated that he had trouble filing a motion to transfer venue, that the Decedent passed away and lived in Alameda County, and that this should be in Alameda County. Mr. Dimitriou also stated that he made efforts to communicate with Ms. Tussey about venue being in Alameda, that Ms. Tussey would not agree and that he filed his motion on Friday with a hearing date of January 21, 2021. Ms. Tussey stated that the Decedent is at a crematory and waiting to be buried, that Mr. Dimitriou's motion is defective and urged the Court to address revocation of Vincent's power of attorney. Ms. Tussey also stated that the power of attorneys were signed at the skilled nursing facility and do not have the required witnesses, that Vincent is asserting the power of attorney authority over the Spouse of the Decedent and that the Attorney-in Fact lives in San Francisco. In addition, Ms. Tussey stated that her petition was filed here because they became aware that the conservatorship was filed here and it was dismissed, and that Francine was desperate. Mr. Dimitriou stated that a conservatorship petition for the Petitioner's Mother is still pending in Alameda, that the Mother lives in Alameda, and that the Decedent died and lived in Alameda. The Court stated that it is not prepared to go forward on Ms. Tussey's petition until it is satisfied that venue is appropriate. Ms. Tussey once again urged the Court to issue a temporary order of revocation. Mr. Dimitriou stated that he stands by his representation that they will hold off with cremation. The Petition for Relief Under Probate Code 4541, etc. is continued to January 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
